description_long: This is a heavily corroded and weathered hammer, likely made of iron or steel, with a thick, irregular layer of rust and mineral deposits covering most of its surface. The head is blocky and roughly rectangular, with no visible claw or peen, suggesting it may be a basic sledge or masonry hammer. The handle is short, straight, and appears to be made of the same metal as the head, with no separate grip or ergonomic shaping. The entire object has a rough, pitted texture, with significant surface degradation and flaking, indicating long-term exposure to the elements. There are no visible markings, logos, or decorative features, and the hammer's original surface is almost entirely obscured by corrosion. This object would likely be found in an abandoned or outdoor urban environment, such as a construction site, scrapyard, or neglected garage.
description: A heavily rusted, corroded metal hammer with a blocky head and short, straight handle, covered in rough mineral deposits and surface pitting.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen from the side, showing the full length of the handle and the blocky, irregularly shaped head with extensive rust and surface roughness.
description_view_1: A narrow end-on view, revealing the thin profile of the handle and the compact, uneven head, with corrosion obscuring finer details.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, highlighting the thick, crusted head and the straight, corroded handle, with more pronounced surface texture and flaking.
description_view_3: Another end-on view, similar to view 1, showing the handle's width and the head's irregular, pitted end.
attribute_handle_type: Straight iron handle, short and heavily corroded, with a rough, pitted surface and no grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Blocky, rectangular iron head, heavily rusted and encrusted, with no visible claw or peen; likely a basic sledge or masonry type.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Extensive rust, pitting, flaking, and mineral deposits covering the entire surface; severe corrosion and surface degradation.
